reviews.ownerResponse:
Please be aware that I had nothing to do with any property damage claim that you had. That was handled by you or your insurance company that dealt directly with the other driver’s insurance company. Property damage claims are almost always handled separately from other claims. I had no involvement in reviewing a market valuation report of your vehicle that was completed by the adjuster in determining the value of your vehicle. I had no involvement in accepting or signing off on the amount that the insurance company agreed to pay for your vehicle. You can confirm this by calling your insurance company and the other driver’s insurance company.

NOBODY CAN FORCE YOU TO ACCEPT A SETTLEMENT AMOUNT.

Richard Dean had a judgment against me I wasn't aware had turned into a judgment till I went to sell my deceased Mothers home. He told me he would leave me enough to move myself and my disabled child. He demanded answers to very personal information, not just from me but via my realtor. Repeatedly drove past my home telling the realtor he wanted to see who I had here. I have felt extremely unsafe and haven't been sleeping well. He left me with $1,000 which isn't enough to move. The lies this man tells are unreal. His reviews from both former employees and debtors tell a story about him. He is a scary man. So if you buy a car from 2nd Chance Auto and it breaks down in 2 weeks, know you'll end up dealing with Richard Dean who will leave you homeless.

I have found a large group of people he has also treated this way....demeaning, dismissive, rude. To this day he refuses to email me paperwork of the bill.

We are all filing complaints to the State Bar and some are signing warrants for his felony stalking.

Amanda Lewis- Yarbrough can you please reach out to me. I know of a similar issue in which recordings and documents have been gathered in reference to illegal actions. We most definitely need to talk and find out if there are others before contacting the BAR.

I am extremely disturbed by my recent experience with Attorney Dean who placed a lien on my home without my knowledge or consent, allegedly on behalf of Zelda Dental. Neither I nor my ex-husband have *ever* been to this dental office, and we have no outstanding debts related to them.

When I contacted the attorney's office to clarify this serious mistake, I was met with extreme rudeness and an unprofessional attitude. Rather than listening to the situation or attempting to resolve the error, the attorney was demeaning, dismissive, and condescending throughout the call. I was treated with complete disrespect as a homeowner trying to understand why my property was being wrongfully targeted.

Placing a lien on someone’s property is a serious legal action and should not be taken lightly—especially without proper investigation or communication. This kind of negligence and behavior is completely unacceptable and unethical. I have begun the process of formally reporting him to the State Bar for misconduct and will also be notifying the local judiciary and relevant authorities about this situation.

If you're considering hiring this attorney, I strongly urge you to reconsider. Based on my experience, I would be very concerned about both his ethics and professionalism.

I called, once again told them I am blind, once again made sure they had the correct number, and offered to pay the 200 in interest from nearly two years ago that, again, I did not know I owed. This place is horrible at communication, does not honor communication preferences of disabled individuals, or at least did not in my case, and are greedy to make a buck. They could have resolved this case without resorting to a garnishment. Further, they could have reached out sooner, without threatening garnishment about the interest. reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for calling our office. We charge a consultation fee to talk to an attorney for all criminal cases, regardless of what charges you're facing so that we can devote the proper amount of time to understanding the circumstances surrounding your case, to discuss possible defenses, and to fully explain what you should expect moving forward. We don't do drive-by consultations, which is what you might get for free elsewhere. Sometimes you really do get what you pay for.

I regret that we were unable to help you, but we truly wish you the best outcome possible, whomever you choose to handle your case.

reviews.text: I hate to leave a lower review. Although I willingly paid for a consultation and  he told me he was too busy to take me on as a client he did instruct me with some documents. the attorney offered to Look back over the documents free of charge seeing as how I’d already paid the consultation fee. I showed up on the agreed date and time for him to look over the documents. No one was at the office. I called and left a voicemail no correspondence thereafter. It’s been a month later and still nothing.
reviews.ownerResponse: Ouch! We are never too busy to take your case, but we may decline to accept a case if your expectations exceed our ability to meet your expectations.  We prefer to be honest with you rather than to make claims that we cannot fulfill.  The details you've laid out here are not like us at all, and if I let this slip I am so sorry. Please call me (334-819-7111) and we'll get whatever paperwork you have squared away. We'll  also refund any consultation fee rather than to leave you unhappy. UPDATE: Still no word from Cici, but we never say "we do not have time." We are honest in all that we do and if a case is very time consuming or the prospective client expects results that we cannot deliver, we do tell them if their case requires more time than we can devote to it. We are streight shooters and if the truth is unwanted, its best to seek other counsel. We do wish Cici the best and pray that she'll become more forgiving because as a business owner herself she should know that none of us is perfect and we all make mistakes.

reviews.text:
I was hit in a car accident a couple of years ago and my personal attorneys were pep and Tyler Sawyer. They were amazing! I wished I would've stayed with them in the car accident that I was in, not long after that, because I was hit by a big company car and had extremely horrible service with another attorney. Having issues with a whole other attorney, I reached back out to Tyler and pep for legal advice in my situation, and to my surprise, they actually responded very quickly with their input.
About a year ago, I was rear ended, yet again, and I reached out, directly, to Pep and Tyler, who were happy to take me on! They schedule initial required appointments, they call to check in with us, not only to keep us updated, but also because they wanted to know about my well being. They are beyond amazing at what they do- I was having a problem with the hospital that I went to after my accident, and they explained to me why the hospital was in the wrong, so they reached out to the hospital and sent them an email with laws in it and explained how the hospital had done wrong, so before sending in a demand, the hospital had to drop the 2800 that would've gone to the hospital, and even talked with my physical therapist and succeeded at having them drop their total down as well.
I haven't met any lawyers who were as dedicated, genuinely cared of the well being of their clients, a real expert at how the law works. I highly recommend Pep and Tyler Sawyer, and if I am ever involved in another accident again, my permanent choice in lawyers will forever, permanently be the Sawyers!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience with us. We deeply cherish your kind words and are glad we could assist you with your legal needs.

reviews.text: Big machine out to satisfy themselves. I was diagnosed with a fractured vertebrae in my neck after an accident involving an 18 wheeler. There public response was they feel so bad, contact them and let’s see if they can come up with a solution. I tried contacting them several time, only to be ignored. Big machine and forced bill board smile used to deceive. Do your homework, find a firm that’s not chasing ambulances.

I had a massive panic attack and barely managed to call and see what was wrong. I was met with more excuses and to this day I do not have the DEED ON MY HOME. I will be seeking advice to review this matter and advise me how to proceed.

I knew instantly with his decades of experience and dedication to his work that Mr. Mahaney was the person I wanted to represent me. From my experience with Mr. Mahaney he was worth every single penny. Not only was I able to continue my day-to-day functioning I was able to enter a program which will allow my charge to be ultimately dropped upon its completion. I feel that Mr. Mahaney's years of experience and devotion to his work is what led me to this extremely positive outcome. In my opinion I say look no further for an attorney and consult with Mr. Mahaney.

reviews.text:
I began working with Mr. Matthews around October 2022. During his representation, communication was minimal. His assistant frequently contacted me, stating she was unable to obtain my medical records. Last month, she informed me that I would need to contact the local hospital myself to get the records, as she was experiencing difficulties. I assume she eventually obtained them, as the defendant provided a settlement offer.

Mr. Matthews did not clearly explain what to expect and pressured me to accept an offer, claiming it was the maximum amount possible for this case. Yesterday, he called to ask how much I was willing to accept. As my attorney, I expected him to advocate for the best outcome for his client. I told him I needed to call him back. When I tried calling, there was no answer, so I emailed him the amount I was considering.

Instead of explaining that the lawsuit was filed in a district with a specific limit, he withdrew from the case and then told me the maximum amount I could receive, stating that my request was unrealistic. Had he communicated this from the beginning, I would have understood the limitations. There was a clear communication barrier, and he should be more considerate of his clients' needs.

He accepted my case and had it for  about and year meet with me once.  I had been talking to Jessica that worked with him and she was persistent with getting things done. I received and email yesterday saying he no longer wants my case
reviews.ownerResponse: After we reviewed the file and the other materials we obtained in our investigation, we were unable to pursue her claim and we wish her the best. It was an honor to represent her and investigate her claims for her.

reviews.text: Has conflict with insurance companies that adversely affect client rights.
reviews.ownerResponse: First and foremost, it was my honor and privilege to represent Mr. Tompkins for a time for his work injury case; however, I HAVE NOT AND DO NO REPRESENT, NOR AM I ASSOCIATED WITH, ANY INSURANCE COMPANIES. I regularly sue them and their clients for compensation or monetary damages for my clients’ lawful claims for personal injuries, wrongful death, work injuries and other claims. I was sorry that Mr. Tompkins chose to hire another attorney but I understood that it is a personal decision and respected his position. His case was challenging due to a number of issues that came up that had to be addressed. Due to the time it took due to the difficult issues, he ultimately decided to seek another attorney for pursuing the case. I was happy to oblige and provided all information to him to help him pursue his case with new counsel. I want my clients to be as comfortable with my representation as they are with their own medical doctors. If they want a second opinion or wish to seek other counsel, I completely understand that it is a very real and personal decision for them as well as a business decision. I am unsure of the ultimate result of his case as he hired other counsel. It is my goal for each client to be satisfied with their results however with practicing law, that is difficult and realistically impossible in every single case since I have been practicing law over 22 years. I have the utmost respect for Mr. Tompkins and wish him the best.

reviews.text: Great firm. Great communication.
Excellent staff and excellent service.
It didn't help us at all.

What I experienced here has all the symptoms of a document mill. These people charge $400/hr for a consultation. For that price, I am expecting expertise. I did not get that. What I got was an unprofessional looking person who was not only unable to answer my direct question, but actually gave me information that was objectively incorrect.

I repeat: If I hadn't read the USCIS website thoroughly myself beforehand, if I had taken this person's advice, my family may have faced dire consequences.

The entire experience: The attorney was late (even though the office was empty). I was taken into a room with a big projector screen. The person sat me down and attempted to start reading off a scripted sheet of questions. I informed the person I was not at the beginning of the process, and since my money had only bought half an hour, time was of the essence. I came here to get my question answered.

When I asked it, the attorney was obviously unprepared and began stalling and frantically scrolling through the USCIS website on the projector. They stopped on a piece of information and quoted it as the answer to my question, but without going through the rest of the page. Because I knew what was on the rest of the page, I knew for a fact this person was just winging it, trying not to look stupid, and ended up giving me an incomplete picture of my situation and advice that was objectively false.

Now we get into the document milling: This person then began to gaslight me as if I can't fill out my name on a form. This is despite the fact that all of my forms were correctly filled out to this point. The reason for me coming in wasn't because of incomplete or incorrect forms. I was quoted $1000 for a single form; I shudder to think how much they'd charge for someone who needed a 485, 130, 130A, 131, etc filled out.

If I could give 0 stars, I would. Consult here at your own risk. And watch these 5 star "reviews" with no details; may be in need of a validity check.

I had to do nearly everything for myself; they never advised me as to what I needed to do to prepare for my case. No video calls, nothing. I was expecting more for all the money I paid. A lot of money for hardly anything.

I highly recommend them.

We recently hired Mahaley Paulk to represent us in a legal dispute. She did a fantastic job, surpassing our expectations. Being the first time involved in a situation like this, we were frazzled, disoriented, and afraid.

Maheley was very empathetic and took time to patiently explain the law and the process we needed to follow, which brought relief and instilled confidence in us that someone had our back, that we were in good hands. Throughout the legal dispute, she was thorough, persistent, determined, and proactively checked in with us, keeping us informed every step of the way. The outcome of the dispute was favorable to us with unexpectedly large remedies.

We are delighted with Mahaley’s work. Not only did she demonstrate mastery of the law, but more importantly she was compassionate and empathetic, which went a long way in building trust and peace of mind for our family.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for this thoughtful and detailed review. We know how overwhelming it can be to face a legal dispute for the first time, and we’re truly honored that you trusted Mahaley to walk alongside you through it.

She is deeply committed to making sure her clients understand what’s happening, feel supported, and never feel alone in the process. Your words about her empathy, communication, and determination mean a great deal — that combination of strong advocacy and genuine compassion is exactly what we strive to provide every day.

We’re so glad the outcome brought relief and peace of mind to your family. Thank you again for your trust and for taking the time to share your experience.

She was thorough, responsive, and consistently one step ahead, making sure we understood our options and felt supported throughout a very stressful time. Her professionalism and dedication made a difficult situation far more manageable, and the outcome reflected the hard work she put into the case. Mahaley was supported by an incredible paralegal, Angela.

We couldn’t have asked for a better advocate. Highly recommend.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We know how frightening and overwhelming it is when your child is involved in a serious accident, and we’re truly honored that you trusted Mahaley and our team to help your family through it.

reviews.text:
As a father who deeply loves his children and prioritizes their well-being, finding Isaak Law was invaluable during my divorce. Their team helped me navigate through heightened emotions and ego, keeping me focused on what truly mattered - fairness and my children's best interests.
The professionalism and dedication of this firm exceeded my expectations. Their approach is straightforward: if you're honest, follow their guidance, and provide information promptly, they will help you achieve the best possible outcome. In a legal system that often favors mothers, it's refreshing to find attorneys who effectively advocate for fathers while maintaining objectivity.
Special recognition goes to Cristina and Nicole, whose exceptional work reminded me of a superhero duo. They were thorough, compassionate, and tenacious in pursuing a fair resolution. The outcome of my case was equitable and unbiased, exactly what I hoped for.
I wholeheartedly recommend Isaak Law to any father seeking fair representation in family court. They deliver results for clients who approach the process with integrity and good intentions.

On behalf of the Cheatham family, we would like to express our deepest gratitude to Attorney Virgil Ford for your outstanding legal representation of our family member, Tuskegee police officer Captain Tommy Cheatham. Thanks to your swift intervention and expert defense, the charges against him were dismissed.
In December 2023, Captain Cheatham was charged with possessing a firearm with an altered serial number in Montgomery County. Your strategic legal approach not only led to the dismissal of the charges but also revealed that the firearm’s serial number was intact, ultimately proving his innocence.
Your professionalism, dedication, and expertise were crucial in securing this victory, and we cannot thank you enough. You’ve truly been a hero to our family, bringing both relief and justice during a challenging time.
We will forever be grateful for your hard work and the exceptional support you provided.

I was an employee of the State Alabama for over 32 years when I filed my case with the EEOC in Birmingham.  Although I had the right to be free from discrimination and harassment based on my identity as a member of a protected class. I was denied a promotion, and subjected to unfair treatment based on my race. I hired Attorney Virgil Ford because he was highly recommended by former Judge Tracy McCooey.

In the beginning, Mr Ford was  professional, charismatic, articulate and very persuasive. Later, Mr. Ford made me aware that he had been approached for my files by an attorney, Timothy “Bo” Offord, a former attorney with the Medicaid office. Mr Offord currently works with the Attorney Generals (AGs) office. Attorney Ford indicated to me at one time they lived in close proximity to each other. At that time, I wasn’t concerned with regards to Attorney Offord’s reaching out to Attorney Ford because I had confidence in his integrity.

I kept submitting the daily acts of retaliation on my job to Attorney Ford. However, for some unknown reason, he stopped responding to my calls, texts, emails, etc. Although Attorney Ford signed the Letter of Representation for EEOC, he also failed to respond to their calls, emails, voicemail, etc. I had no other option but to terminate his services and  I requested the return of my case files. On one occasion, I called him from a friend’s phone, he finally answered but pretended he couldn’t talk with me until he pulled my case file. He never returned my call or my files.

To this day, Attorney Ford has refused to return my EEOC files to me, which leads me to believe he may have sold my case files to my former employees. Mr. Ford undermined my confidence in his integrity, he has clearly violated my rights as a former client. Consequently, he should be disbarred for engaging in unethical or illegal practices. Regrettably I sought Attorney Ford for remedies for civil rights violations in my workplace to correct a discriminatory act or policy and to be compensated as a victim monetarily for these violations. Instead, I was oppressed and violated by my own lawyer. He should be held accountable for his actions/lack of actions. Therefore, I highly DO NOT recommend the Law Office of Hampton and Ford, especially Attorney Virgil Ford.
You don’t want a church boy trying to keep you out of the dog house. You want someone who lives to win. Unorthodox yes, but he’s a powerhouse.

reviews.text:
For most of my life, I worked hard, raised my family, paid my bills, maintained good credit, and purchased many necessities on credit to support my family. I never imagined I'd have to file for bankruptcy or be unable to pay my bills.
Then, the 50-year-old company I'd worked at for years was sold to a competitor who laid off most employees. Subsequently, there was a downturn in jobs across the U.S. My savings depleted, bills accumulated, and creditors began pursuing me. As if that weren't enough, my wife filed for divorce, demanding the lifestyle she was accustomed to. I faced attorney fees, mounting penalties, interest, and fees from all directions. It felt like the weight of the world was pressing down on me while I struggled to stay afloat in the middle of an ocean.
Filing for bankruptcy became my only viable option. I consulted several attorneys who made me question their qualifications. Finally, I spoke with the Bond & Botes Law Office. They were approachable, knowledgeable, and professional where it mattered most. They helped me navigate an impossible debt situation and guided me through filing the bankruptcy that suited my circumstances.
I never wanted to file for bankruptcy, but when life didn't go as planned, Bond & Botes helped me through an extremely challenging time. Everyone's life is different, and we all have our own stories. If you find yourself living in a financial nightmare, know that you have rights. I highly recommend you call Bond & Botes.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for sharing your powerful story. We know it’s not easy to talk about difficult times, and we’re truly honored to have been able to support you during such a challenging chapter in your life. At Bond & Botes, we understand that life doesn’t always go as planned, and we’re committed to helping good people get the fresh start they deserve. Your strength and resilience are inspiring, and we’re grateful for your trust in us.

— The Bond & Botes Law Offices Team

reviews.text: The go to law firm for probate filing. Ed and Kayla are top notch.

Probate can be complicated and daunting.  We were referred to the Parish Law Firm by our closing agent when the seller's probate process stalled and our closing date was missed.  In a matter of days they had the Probate process back on track!  Madison was professional, responsive and a pleasure to work with.  She took the time to explain steps, timelines and requirements with cheerful patience.  These knowledgeable, experienced professionals ensured the probate process had no further delays, and the closing was completed.  Definitely recommend for all Probate needs in AL.

Choosing the Parish Law Firm in Montgomery, AL was one of the best decisions my family and I made when it came to drafting our will. Ed and his team were incredibly knowledgeable, professional, and attentive throughout the entire process. Their expertise guided us seamlessly through what could have been a daunting task. The level of service we received was truly above average, and their dedication to ensuring our needs were met exceeded our expectations. I wholeheartedly recommend the Parish Law Firm to anyone seeking exceptional legal assistance. Thank you, Ed, and the entire team, for your invaluable support!

After 30 years as an official court reporter, if I ever need a real estate attorney, I call The Parish Law Firm.  They are definitely the most knowledgeable, and I recommend them to all of my family and friends.
Professionalism, knowledge, and speed 100/10! Even if he's busy!

The Parish law firm did a excellent job handling my deceased husbands probate case my sons and I were treated with the utmost respect kept abreast of the progress alway always answered any questions or concerns in a timely fashion

I can honestly tell you there is not enough room to write for everything that has gone wrong . I will start with this . I have been in probate court for 2 years now just trying to get my dads house deeded over to mine and my sisters name . I can tell you I have lost the house thru foreclosure and everything in it . My dad was a 100 percent military disabled veteran and all that was owed on the house was 4,900 dollars . A investor came in because Ed got a useless order that we waited 15 months to get from circuit court for him to tell us later that that order that was given was not valid . I was told today that we will probably loose the case on Monday because I need to get a foreclosure lawyer and probably the best one available. I have a court date 8-19-2022  at 10 am to see if the judge will show me and my family mercy . The truth is if you just need documents printed up I would look elsewhere because based on my experience my family have been treated the worse . I have contacted the Alabama attorney generals office to file a complaint but have not heard back yet . No matter what I have been told that it is only a disciplinary hearing to get to the facts of any misconduct but my family home will still be lost. I have gotten no apology and for some reason all he keeps asking me is WHAT DO YOU WANT ME to do ?? Completely devastated . All my dads medals from Japan , Korea, etc all gone . My dad was the only child so all my grandparents belonging sand family tree !! gone . I am praying others have had this experience or something similar because I will need help bringing him to justice . Please look elsewhere to handle your family needs .

Ed and Sarah were recommended to us by another attorney. They helped us with probate case and a related civil matter.  Ed and his team were very knowledgeable/experienced, friendly and provided excellent service. This is a highly ethical law practice that you can trust to handle your most imported legal matters.
Ed Parish and Valerie Cain Owens are the best! They were professional and so helpful to me and my family during a challenging time.

Been waiting for a year for a call back from Ed. I was calling monthly, and no return calls. My case is time sensitive because the offender is almost 100 years old, and my mom that’s suing the accused is in her hospice bed.
The two conversations I had with Ed were great. I spent hours sending him all the paperwork needed. A family friend attorney recommended him to us. Don’t contact if you need help in a timely manner .. or help in general . I feel sorry for his secretary telling me time after time she would have Ed return my call knowing he wouldn’t.  Every month that passes I’ll leave a poor review .
They helped with allot of things
They were efficient, friendly, and accommodating.
Best firm ever!!! Settled 2 estates, such wonderful, knowledgeable professionals. They care about you. Highly recommended.

After I walked into his office to schedule an appointment to make a Final Will, Mr. Parish asked me some questions, then said, "We can knock this will out today!" Within hours, my Final Will was completed. He worked within my budget because he stressed on how important my Final Will is and wanted it completed asap. Mr. Parish and his team is very fluid with positive energy which made my elementary legal questions easy to ask without any frustrated lawyer feedback.

Attorney Parrish and his staff are very professional, knowledgeable and attentive.  He's not the kind of attorney that will just take your money, if it doesn't look like it's in your favor he will tell you.  I would definitely recommend the Parrish Law firm to family and friends.  Excellent!

Parker Johnston handled my case competently, professionally and truthfully without breaking the bank. Everything I was told to expect happened as he explained it. My case went smoothly and the results were excellent. Parker has a compassionate, no-nonsense approach that was greatly appreciated by this client. My calls were returned promptly and if he was scheduled to give me a follow up call, he did. Legal issues can be daunting and frightening. Parker makes the process easier to get through. Thank you, Johnston and Daniel!

Mr. Johnston went over & above everything we expected in an attorney.  He was thorough, compassionate, patient, & professional in every way.  We could not have made it through our ordeal without his help.

Parker Johnston represented our family in a bitter divorce case-one that involved a child being taken out of state and false charges being filed against the father.  Parker immediately took over this nightmare for  us and the courts ordered the mother to bring the child back to this state.  Parker battled unimaginable obstacles and was awesome in drawing out the truth in deposition and gathering evidence that proved to be in favor of the father.  This case went back to court three times and each time Parker got the father more well-deserved time with the child.  Divorce is a terrible thing but having an experienced, dependable, caring attorney is the only way to handle it.

Parker represented me in a heavily contested divorce. I write this to warn you, if your spouse has wronged you and especially wronged your children, like using their innocence and love for you as a tool or a weapon against you. Parker is a machine that never seems to sputter or run out of fuel. However, I'd imagine it would go quite the opposite way if you are the one that got over on your kids and spouse, so I don't believe Parker would be the right one for you to hire for that. I won, and my kids won too and I was still able to afford a steak dinner on Saturday nights when I felt like getting back out there to start all over again. Good Luck, however luck may have less to do with it if Parker is on your team.

reviews.text:
I contacted McDowell, Faulk & Shirley, LLC because my late sister had listed one of their attorneys as her medical emergency contact. I explained the situation to the receptionist, but she was unhelpful and dismissive. As a result, I was misdirected in my search for information and wasted $10 pursuing a dead end. Only after expressing my frustration did the receptionist provide updated contact information for J. Pendergrass.
I have since reached out to Mr. Pendergrass, but he has stopped responding for reasons unknown. Unfortunately, neither law firm has offered any meaningful assistance in helping me understand how I can support matters following my sister’s passing. While I now believe I’ve identified the correct contacts, their refusal to communicate—going so far as to shut off the phone—has only added to an already difficult situation.

We sincerely apologize for the difficulties you've encountered. reviews.text:
As a loving and deeply concerned mother, watching my daughter face a serious federal conspiracy drug charge was one of the most painful experiences of my life. We were terrified, overwhelmed, and unsure where to turn—until we found Attorney Terry Luck. From the very beginning, Mr. Luck treated us with compassion and honesty, while showing an exceptional command of the law. He never made false promises, but he gave us something we desperately needed: hope. He worked tirelessly on my daughter’s case, examined every detail, and stood by her with the kind of strength and clarity that gave us confidence through every step of the process.

What touched me most was how he truly saw my daughter as a whole person—not just a case file. He was able to convey her story with humanity and depth, and thanks to his remarkable advocacy, she received probation—a result we were told was nearly impossible. I believe with all my heart that Mr. Luck changed the course of her life. If you are a parent praying for someone to truly fight for your child, I cannot say this more clearly: Terry Luck is the one you want in that courtroom.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Debra, for your heartfelt review. We're honored to have been able to support you and your daughter during such a challenging time. Your trust in our advocacy means the world to us.

reviews.text:
As a retired Naval officer and a father, I’ve learned the value of integrity, dedication, and the ability to perform under pressure, qualities that I saw firsthand in Attorney Terry Luck. My daughter was facing a serious federal conspiracy drug charge, a situation that felt overwhelming and, frankly, hopeless. From our first meeting, Mr. Luck was honest about the challenges ahead but also confident in his ability to fight for the best possible outcome. His calm demeanor, deep knowledge of federal law, and strategic approach to building her defense gave our family a sense of hope we hadn’t felt in months.

What truly stood out was Mr. Luck's relentless advocacy and ability to communicate the human side of my daughter’s story to the court. Against all odds, he argued her case so effectively that she received probation — an outcome we had been told was highly unlikely. I firmly believe this result wouldn’t have been possible without his expertise and determination. As a father, I can’t express enough gratitude for what he’s done for my daughter and our family. If your loved one’s future is on the line, and you need someone who will fight with skill, heart, and unwavering commitment, Terry Luck is the attorney you want standing between them and the full weight of the justice system.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Donnie, for your heartfelt review. We're honored to have been able to support your family during such a challenging time. Your trust in our dedication and expertise means the world to us.

reviews.text:
Terry Luck Achieved the Impossible – A True Lifesaver

I was staring down 46 to 57 months in federal prison for a serious drug conspiracy charge. The odds of walking away with just probation? Nearly zero. Federal court isn’t forgiving — especially in conspiracy cases. But then came Terry Luck.

Terry didn’t just step in as my lawyer — he became my shield, my voice, and my relentless advocate. He understood every angle of the law, built a strategy with surgical precision, and went toe-to-toe with the federal government to defend my future.

The result? A jaw-dropping outcome: 5 days time served and 3 years of probation. That kind of break is almost unheard of in federal cases like mine. But Terry made it happen.

His team is just as extraordinary. Lauren, his paralegal, kept everything running like clockwork and treated me with patience and dignity every step of the way. Hannah helped ensure no detail was missed — they worked like a well-oiled machine, all fighting for me.

Terry Luck didn’t just reduce my sentence. He gave me a second chance at life when the system was ready to take it away. If you’re facing impossible odds, there is no one more capable or more committed than Terry.

He is a once-in-a-lifetime lawyer. I owe him everything.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for those kind words Hillary. But the Lord and your family deserve the credit. I was just honored to be there with you.

I requested (twice) guidance on what needed to be submitted to her to get an agreement started...she failed to provide that guidance on both occasions. However, she did state what her fee would be for the agreement...a flat rate fee.  When I questioned why she states she charges a "flat rate fee", yet she includes verbiage stating there could be other charges/expenses, she noted I could make an appointment to come in and discuss the issue.  As I told her, I'm not interested in paying an hourly rate to discuss her confusing fee schedule.  Rather, I went to another attorney who was not only considerably less expensive, he provided a template to follow for an agreement and didn't charge for the 45 minutes I spent in his office.  He has my business...

Mr Yates helped me get on disability. My issues are mostly psychological and not physical. With his help in preparation I was able to be successful in my hearing despite my severe anxiety disorder.

JR Yates handled my Social Security Disability claim for about 2 years.  Initially I filed a claim on my own, was denied.  I then contact JR as he was recommended to me by a good many people.  From there he was a God send!  He handled everything!  My case was unique and out of the ordinary, yet because of JR's extensive experience in SSDI he was able to obtain and immense amount of medical records for many providers and was able to pull together a compelling case.  I was denied again, JR petitioned the court for an appeal.  Had the appeal, got very complicated and Judge said she could deny me right there on the spot.  But JR once again gave a compelling argument and the Judge said she would grant another hearing in 90 days.  Jr was very well prepared for the next hearing and I was awarded FULLY FAVORABLE! I thank God for putting JR in my path and without him I surely would not have gotten this much needed benefit.  It took in total over a 3 year process. But JR never gave up even though I grew weary!  He pushed me to continue.  I WOULD NOT use anyone else for a disability claim attorney than Mr. JR. Yates.  Not only he is super knowledgeable and experienced in this field, he is so easy to work with!  He always returns calls, text, emails, etc. He is kind and compassionate and reassuring.  He sat with me as I cried many times. I never wanted to file for disability, I would much rather have good health and work.  But that has not been the case in life.  JR understand it ALL!.  He's just amazing! Thank your JR! You made a HUGE difference in my life and I will FOREVER be grateful for you! God Bless you!
Amazing lawyer who had my back through the whole thing. Really down to earth and just a wonderful person. He made sure I understood everything that was happening and being said about my case.

reviews.text: Hi for the life of me have no earthly idea how this company is getting 4.9 stars in reviews from the start to the current. The customer service has been less superb. I’ve been tossed around from left to right with no answers. I’m begging people to do their jobs, but the fact remains that if I get paid this, sorry lack of compassion law firm would obtain thousands of Dollars out of my money from my pain and suffering and things I’ve been through! I pray the right person read these reviews so I can fire these people! They aren’t compassionate and they don’t want to do their jobs!
reviews.ownerResponse:
We disagree with your characterization of our firm and the services provided.

As attorneys, we are bound by strict confidentiality and ethical rules that prevent us from addressing case-specific matters in a public forum. What we can say is that our team made consistent, good-faith efforts to communicate with you, respond to your requests, and address your concerns throughout the course of our representation.

When it became clear that your expectations could not be met, despite our best efforts, we concluded our representation. While we regret that the relationship ended this way, we do not believe continuing representation under those circumstances would have served either party well.

Brock & Stout has built its reputation over nearly three decades by prioritizing responsiveness, professionalism, and access to quality legal representation. We stand by the work our team performed in this matter and remain committed to providing attentive service to our clients. We try to ensure that each case is handled with the requisite care and empathy; however, that will necessarily mean candor and having to be direct at times when speaking with a claimant. We are very mindful of the fact that our clients are the lifeblood of our practice; therefore, a positive customer experience is our utmost priority. In fact, it is the only reason we have been successful over the last 29 years.

We wish you the best in finding representation that better aligns with your expectations.

Brock & Stout Attorneys at Law
Thanks

As my representatives, I expected more. It was Dolinsky Law Firm that initially approached me to offer their services, and I expected a higher standard of professionalism and dedication to my needs. Ultimately, I was impressed by Victoria's professionalism; following my expression of concerns, she demonstrated a high level of commitment, communicating efficiently and keeping me well-informed throughout the final stages of my case closure, and I regret not having the chance to work with her from the beginning. I was at a red light when a two vehicle collision happened in front of me. One of the vehicles careened off of the other and slammed into me, totaling my car. I had some head, neck, and back injuries requiring PT. Jim worked with me to get my settlement from their insurance company and made sure they came up to the price I wanted instead settling for what they initially offered. I am extremely satisfied and grateful to Jim and his staff & will recommend his services to anyone who needs it.

I was in a no-fault, multi-car accident that totaled my car and just about totaled me. I had to go through close to two years of physical therapy to relearn how to walk, get my neck to once again be able to take the weight of my head, and deal with a complex brain growth caused by the accident. Jim was there for me from day 1 and, in the end, was able to get my full policy. I am extremely grateful to Jim and his staff for their amazing help. For helping me rebuild what was so severely broken in a moment of careless driving. For being a trusted guide through the legal, medical, and insurance aspects of the accident. For not giving up on me when I was at my most vulnerable. You would be hard pressed to find a more upstanding man and a better attorney. If you ever need a personal injury attorney, do yourself a favor and hire Jim. He is truly the best! Thank you, Jim & team! Keep on making this world a better place, a case at a time! Eternally grateful to you ~ A

It’s extremely rare to find attorneys with as much integrity as Attorney Riddle. He handled a case for my 16 year old son and was EXACTLY what he needed. In 2019 I was having some financial issues , especially being in a single parent home. David walked me through the process of bankruptcy (chapter 13) I was in a 5 year plan. Fast fwd to 2024, I was about to be done with the plan and he came to me about my student loans and said he due to new laws passing he might could get my student loans forgiven. This process took about 8 months but the process was worth it. $86,000 was forgiven and I have a new blank slate. David and his staff made this process very understandable and easy. I highly recommend him and his office. He explains everything and is very patient, professional and knowledgeable.
If he could have 10 stars I would give it !!! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.text:
"Strickland and Kendall were instrumental in helping me navigate a complex family law matter. I was facing a challenging divorce and, and I felt completely overwhelmed and lost.
I chose to work with Strickland and Kendall based on several factors.
Firstly, their reputation for expertise in family law was well-regarded in the community. Secondly, I was impressed by the firm's compassionate and client-centered approach. During my initial consultation, I felt heard and understood, which was crucial for me at that time.

The firm provided me with exceptional legal representation. They meticulously reviewed all the relevant documents, conducted thorough investigations, and developed a strong legal strategy tailored to my specific needs. They were always available to answer my questions and address my concerns, providing me with consistent support and guidance throughout the entire process.
The outcome of the case was favorable, exceeding my initial expectations.

The impact on my situation was profound. The stress and anxiety I had been experiencing significantly diminished, allowing me to focus on healing and rebuilding my life. Strickland and Kendall provided me with the peace of mind and confidence I desperately needed during this difficult period. I am incredibly grateful for their professionalism, dedication, and unwavering support."
reviews.ownerResponse: Carlos, thank you so much for the thoughtful words. It means the world to us that we were able to help you so profoundly. reviews.ownerResponse:
We understand how frustrating it is to be injured in an accident only to discover there is no insurance coverage available and share your frustration with the situation. However, we worked diligently on your behalf from day one and did everything in our power to find insurance coverage that could help your situation. While we wish the circumstances could have been different, we want to clarify the timeline of our work on your case.

Your claim was opened the same day you were referred to us. When we learned the at-fault driver had no insurance, we immediately opened an uninsured motorist claim with the insurance carrier for the vehicle you were in—on that very same day. When we received confirmation that uninsured motorist coverage had not been purchased by the owner of that vehicle, we promptly shared this information with you. This confirmation arrived just four days before your review was posted.

While we wish the outcome had been different, the unfortunate reality is that without insurance from the at-fault driver and without uninsured motorist coverage for you or the vehicle you were in, there was no avenue to pursue compensation. This is precisely why we've spent 25 years educating our community about the critical importance of uninsured motorist coverage through articles, public speeches, free insurance policy reviews, and other efforts.

Uninsured motorist coverage protects you when an at-fault driver has no insurance. Had this coverage been in place, your case would have had a different outcome. We strongly encourage everyone reading this to review their auto insurance policies today and add this essential protection if they don't already have it. We remain committed to serving our community and wish you the best in your recovery.

I was unaware of all the necessary things (and legalese) that had to be included in a living trust and she walked me through it all and charged a reasonable fee. I am very well satisfied with the work she did for me and would recommend her whole-heartedly to anyone needing to make decisions regarding living trusts, probate issues, or other end-of-life legal issues. Ms. Comer is the person to see if you want the best professional help available  and who is also empathetic to your needs.

reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Reynolds, we’re sorry we did not meet your expectations.  Many law firms have more than one attorney, and our small firm is no different. Our appointments usually last at least 30 minutes, which allows the attorney to go over the legal options that fit the client’s situation, needs, goals, and expectations.  Keeping an open mind when you meet with a lawyer is the best way to get the most out of your meeting.  Coming into that meeting dead-set against an idea is a sure-fire recipe for disaster.  We regret that we were unable to represent Ms. Reynolds, but her goals and expectations were not achievable through the avenue she wanted to pursue. We fight for our clients every day and pride ourselves in making sure our clients receive legal representation and advice that meets and exceeds their expectations, and we feel the rest of our Google reviews strongly reflect this.

reviews.text:
Embarrassing when you hire a lawyer and they send out a document on your behalf discussing HIPPA laws and it is very clear that the paralegal entrusted to draw up the document isn’t familiar enough with the referenced law to sound intelligent on behalf of their client.

With HIPPA very much in the news recently, I’ll fill everyone in on an important tidbit of info not covered in law school apparently.

HIPPA protections DO NOT apply to everyone that comes in contact with your info.

While “Violations of HIPPA carry substantial penalties” - they only apply to certain medical professionals.

If an entity does not meet the definition of a covered entity, it does not have to comply.

This includes providers such as:
Doctors
Clinics
Psychologists
Dentists
Chiropractors
Nursing Homes
Pharmacies
...but only if they transmit any information in an electronic form in connection with a transaction for which HHS has adopted a standard. or business associate, it does not have to comply with the HIPAA Rules.

If you hire this law firm as “parents (that) have decades of experience in Christian education and youth ministry, and are widely loved and respected in their community "  😂
- I encourage you to fact-check their documents before allowing them to speak on your behalf, lest you sound ridiculously foolish.

(After you’ve paid for the privilege.)

reviews.text:
More than a year ago, my wife and I found ourselves involved in litigation trying to save our home. We had signed a Bond for Title and it had gone horrible wrong. We contacted an attorney and started the process to remove the tenants. We were told this process could take a few months but we should not have a problem.
Unfortunately, that was inaccurate. We were not only having issues with removing the tenants, we were told they were filing claims to sue us and essential steal our home. Our attorney spoke with us for hours and basically told us they, the defendants, had a case. We were also told we should settle this and same a lot of money, a lot of stress, and a lot of time.
Yes, we were told that we should basically allow the very people that failed to pay us for the initial contract, that frequently failed to pay the rent on time or the correct amount and the people that felt they had rights to sale our home were indeed in the driver’s seat and had the leverage.
After a few months, my wife and I decided we needed new representation. We asked around, we conducted research as much as possible and then a name come across that we recognized, Hon. Jerry Blevins. I had met Mr. Blevins several years ago and wish I would have remembered he was a practicing attorney. We reached out to Mr. Blevins and explained our situation. In the initial conversation, we felt for the first time in the months of litigation, we had someone who would defend our rights.
Mr. Blevins immediately sprung into action. He contacted our previous attorney, he contacted the oppositions attorney, he contacted the courts. Mr. Blevins began educating himself on our case and within days, he was the subject matter expert; more so than previous personnel involved in the case form its’ conception. This case had started in District Court and moved to Circuit Court and he completely understand the details in each court.
He not only routinely spoke to our family, he ensured we were continuously updated on any actions pertaining to our case. Mr. Blevins literally was a phone call away at any time. We have spoke with him early morning, during the days, evenings and over the weekends. We were not a burden to him, he genuinely cared.
His actions as an attorney were simply amazing. He knew this case, he studied case law relating to similar cases, he ensured our interest were paramount, and he was the ultimate professional.
Mr. Blevins treated us like family, saved our home and restored our faith in the legal system. He is brilliant, he is fierce and will be the right choice for you, should you need legal representation.

My experience with this Law Firm was a negative, deplorable, horrible and bad, I met with a person named Raymond Hawthorne, he did not reveal that he didn't have the experience in the Property Damage Claim or , that this was a practice of his, he failed to disclose this information. It took approximately over two months for his legal assistant to prepare a document for my Roof Contractor to sign, by the time they had gotten the document ready in December, the Contractor had left his job, and they weren't able to contact him, this sad, unprofessional, and incompetent, because Mr. Hawthorne, was not familiar with Property Damage Claims,and it was obvious because it took over two months to complete a document, apparently, his legal assistant was responsible for completing the document, and she stated that she was not familiar with Property Damage Claim, this firm seems to specialize in serious personal injury claims, and some general however, it does not specify what they are, due to Mr. Hawthorne negligence and failure to prepare a document for my contractor to sign, my contractor has changed jobs, and I don't know how to contact my contractor, Mr. Hawthorne stated that they tried to contact my contractor in December of 2025, and my contractor had already left the job, and this is the reason that they were unable to contact my contractor, however, Mr. Hawthorne and his legal assistant stated that my contractor refused to sign document, this isn't true because Mr. Hawthorne and his legal assistant could not contact my contractor because he had left the Roof Contractor Job, and went to work for another company. I am absolutely astounded and very disappointed and dissatisfied with this poor misrepresentation, I don't know how to contact the Roof Contractor, as a result of Mr. Hawthorne negligence and failure to contact my Roof Contractor in a timely manner, instead of waiting over two months, this could have been prevented. I received a email stating that Mr. Hawthorne would not be representing me because he failed to contact my Roofing Contractor, before he changed jobs. I reiterate, I met with Mr. Hawthorne in October of 2025, and my Roof Contractor didn't leave his job until late December. Due to the excessive, incompetent and unprofessional delay in preparing document for my contractor to sign, I have lost this case as result ofMr. Hawthorne negligence,incompetence, unprofessional lack to acquire a signature before my contractor left job, it is preposterous and sad, because the red flags were there, I called and inquired as to why it was taking so long to get document ready, it is not a excuse.

I called and spoke to the legal assistant informing her that I was extremely disappointed and dissatisfied with the poor service, and she stated she was out for two weeks in December, and my contractor left in December, apparently, they tried to contact my contractor in January, and my contractor had already left, very poor service.

Mr. Hawthorne, called with excuses for their incompetent and unprofessional actions, he was rude and hostile, he stated that he would give me a list of other Attorneys that practice in Property Damage Claims, however, he did not, a huge failure in every thing, he was aware that he was not a Property Damage Attorney, and should have been honest from the beginning, however, he wasn't, this was a gigantic bad experience, I absolutely do not recommend this Law Firm. It has caused me to lose my case, because of his negligence, lackadaiscal attitude, poor performance, incompetence and unprofessional lack of experience in Property Damage Claim, he did not have the experience in this practice, it should have stated the truth and been honest.

reviews.text:
I cannot say enough good things about Julia Collins and the entire team at the Harris Law Firm. From start to finish, they made my uncontested divorce process far more seamless, productive, and quicker than I ever anticipated.

Going through a divorce is never easy—emotionally or logistically—but Julia and her team took the stress off my shoulders in a way that I’ll always be grateful for. Julia was compassionate, highly knowledgeable, and walked me through every step with clarity and professionalism. Her legal assistant, Jan Fagan, was equally impressive—organized, responsive, and always ready to answer my questions no matter how small. Even the notary public played a part in making the entire experience smooth and efficient.

5/5 Stars - Exceptional Representation in Child Custody Case*

I wholeheartedly recommend Julia
for any family law or child custody case. She expertly handled my complex and emotionally charged case with unparalleled professionalism, empathy, and determination.

Julia is a force to be reckoned with in the courtroom - a true powerhouse. Her unwavering confidence, razor-sharp instincts, and exceptional litigation skills make her a formidable opponent. But what truly sets her apart is her willingness to be blunt and honest, always keeping her clients best interests at heart.

After four long years of uncertainty, Julia took on my case and achieved the unthinkable, settling it outside of court in just six months. Her strategic approach, tireless work ethic, and exceptional negotiation skills ensured an outcome that exceeded my expectations.

Her dedication, compassion, and support throughout the process were invaluable. She listened, advised, and fought tirelessly for my rights and, more importantly, my child's well-being.

If you're seeking an attorney who will fiercely advocate for you and your family, look no further. Julia is the ultimate champion. I'm forever grateful for her expertise, guidance, and unwavering commitment to justice.

I have no issue paying for their time, but 1.1K (total) to tell me they won't/can't do it seems like a money grab.  They charged $100 apparently just for reading an email.   Even charge me for talking to their secretary asking if they had made a determination yet after a month of waiting.    They are obviously all and only about the money.   However Vance Law firm was phenomenal in winning a lawsuit for my mom, then without charge or even me asking, helped me resolve the POA issue so my mom's assets can be protected.   And Tolar Law/Prattville is doing the trust for a fixed price. reviews.text: Proceed with caution. I hired Mr. Morris for a custody case and he sold me pretty quickly. The video on his website is superb. I bought it. I read through reviews before I hired him and all seemed great besides a few for lack of communication so I proceeded. Oh the regrets I have now. 5000 dollars later just to go to mediation where he tried to belittle me and rush me to come to an agreement. When I say rush he didn’t even want me thoroughly reading the agreement (great thing I did because there were many mistakes and things left in it that were supposed to be taken out). Thankfully he had a mentor with him learning the ropes because he even tried to lie and say things were said and explained that weren’t. When he looked at his mentor to back him the gentleman politely said that isnt the way I understood it and that isnt what was said. ( I’m sure he will be a great attorney). I really thought I had a great attorney that would go In there ready to fight but wow am I baffled. By far the worse attorney I have ever used. Everything that I got was because it was given to me not because he fought. I do get my child half of the month but still get to pay 700 dollars a month and split any and everything with her mother (one child). He didn’t even ask for paystubs. They went off of a tax return from the prior year which rounded out to her making minimum wage even thought I know 100 percent for a fact she makes double plus minimum wage. Me paying for her insurance didn’t even come into the equation. If I could give this guy less stars I promise you the sky would be black. You do not have to take my advice but I promise you this. I have no reason to lie about anything and I am an open book. I’m sure his method might work and may have worked better had he not been in a rush ti get it over with or Atleast remember things from my case before we got there. He even had the nerve to sit in MY MEDIATION and briefly talk about another case with is mentor and say “that’s a good case”  I was a number and a paycheck and if I could do it all over again I would rather represent myself or pick someone who barely passed the bar to represent me. As I stated before proceed with caution. Any question please feel free to reach out as I stated I am an open book.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Mr. Haney:
I am sorry that you feel that you were rushed. I genuinely try my best to take my time with each client, and give them my very best. When we left that day, I thought that you were happy with the agreement, otherwise you should have told me, and we could have taken our chances with the judge. As I have always said that you have that option. Instead, you chose to settle out of court to provide a good chance for you to get 50/50 custody, and the desired 50/50 custody was achieved for you.
I wish the best for you.
Sincerely,
Jon Morris

reviews.text: Mr. Morris and his Law Firm are truly outstanding. Their resolution of my child support case was superb. Although my case originated in this area, I've since moved back to my home State of Illinois, where the order is being enforced. After years of payments, I proved to my home State that I had already paid a considerable sum above what I was required to.  My home State then stopped all enforcement actions after an administrative judge here, intervened.  A couple of months later the DHS office there, corresponded directly with my employer (no court) with a new withholding order.  I've been fighting this for years.  My child is in his thirties!  Mr. Morris came to my rescue. His attention to detail is amazing. As he reviewed years of my saved correspondence with both States, he was able to spot many discrepancies and omissions.  They were still garnishing my wages, raided my bank account, and were taking my yearly tax returns. They supposedly conducted two account reviews at my request, as required by law, and they couldn't see the discrepancies.  Mr. Morris set up a court date for me.  On the day of the hearing, he had set up such a strong case, that I didn't have to testify, and my case was dismissed!  If anyone needs a highly knowledgeable, professional Attorney, not to mention how friendly the entire staff is, I highly recommend the Morris Law Firm!!!

I highly recommend Jon for any child support/custody case. He is very detailed and knowledgeable and killed it in court. I have had other lawyers but none compare to what Jon was able to do for me. If you need help I suggest setting up a consultation you wont be disappointed.

reviews.text: Listen to the bad HONEST reviews!! I gave them the benefit of the doubt and ended up wasting thousands of dollars. The communication is awful. They don’t tell you what to expect, what information they need about your case, nothing. Which results in you having to ask those extra questions and then charging you more money. They charge for any and every little thing and do not do their job. You’ll be charged hundreds of dollars for creating and submitting a counter proposal YOURSELF and then charged more for them to “review” your messages, but once you get someone on the phone they will tell you “they have not even looked at the message because they didn’t have time.” Every time you speak to someone you’ll have to retell them your entire case. They give you a 5 days notice of your event via the portal notification, then you’ll have to call multiple times to actually speak to someone in order find out what you’re suppose to do, if you’ll be able to speak to someone before the event, or if they want to know what you’d be asking for prior to the event. The opposing counsel will put in multiple request and all they will do is submit the don’t to the portal. They never call you to inform you of the request, or ask you if you want to submit a counter proposal. You will have to call 10+ times in one week to get someone to actually speak to you. Save your money and find someone who does their job and communicates properly.
reviews.ownerResponse: I am sorry you feel this way but don't see you in our files. Feel free to contact Mrs Tompkins to discuss your concerns or at least let us know who you are so we can review your matter for improvement. We obviously can not control outcome of court but as the senior attorney, I can't fix something I don't know about.
